scene.description: A vibrant social media announcement graphic for a street food menu reveal. Dark dramatic food photography style.

background.type: dark charcoal surface, almost black, with dramatic overhead spotlight creating pools of warm light
background.lighting: moody overhead spotlight, warm golden tones, professional food photography style with visible steam and texture
background.atmosphere: premium street food, late-night food event energy

food_arrangement.style: overhead flat lay view of Mexican street food items artfully arranged on the dark surface

items.type: a thick juicy smash burger with melted cheese, lettuce, and special sauce on a brioche bun, with a pile of golden crispy fries next to it
items.visual: steam rising, cheese melting, looks incredibly appetizing

items.type: five golden crispy rolled taquitos standing upright, deep-fried and crunchy, served on a plate
a heaping plate of loaded fries covered in seasoned ground beef and dripping melted cheese with green garnish
items.visual: cheese pull visible, messy and delicious

items.type: a bright orange mango drink in a clear cup with a chili-dusted rim and lime wedge
items.visual: vibrant orange color, condensation on cup

items.type: a red spicy michelada drink in a tall glass with lime garnish and chili seasoning on rim

text_elements.position: center of the image, overlaying the food
text_elements.style: massive bold white uppercase text with a subtle black drop shadow for readability
text_elements.font_style: ultra bold condensed sans-serif like Bebas Neue or Impact
text_elements.size: very large, dominant, takes up most of the width

text_elements.text: 6 ITEMS YOU CAN'T GET ANYWHERE ELSE IN ATLANTA
text_elements.style: bright green (#16a34a) bold uppercase text, clearly readable against the dark background
white text with a green checkmark, small clean badge

branding.overall_style: premium street food meets luxury product drop. Think Supreme drop energy applied to food. NOT corporate, NOT stock-photo. Raw and appetizing.

must_include: the subtitle text MUST be large enough to read on a phone screen
